violations.text: The floor and near stairs in 3-bay sink area has some rodent droppings. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 02/02/2026

The chlorine sanitizer concentration for the sanitizing cycle of the dishwasher was 0 parts per million (ppm). Chlorine sanitizing solutions must be between 50-100 ppm. PIC is to properly sanitize equipment in the 3 bay sink until unit is serviced/repaired. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 03/21/2024

The presence of rodent activity is not controlled as evidenced by rodent droppings located underneath 3-bay sink, next to prep unit in kitchen and behind the bar area. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. New Violation. Correct By: 01/14/2026
Ice wand located in the freezer was not in good repair (cracked). PIC voluntarily disposed of ice wand. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/14/2026

At the time of re-inspection, the dish machine was not dispensing chemical sanitizer for proper sanitizing. Per the PIC, equipment and utensils are being properly sanitized in the 3 bay sink until dish machine is fully serviced. (Per the PIC, a part is needed and in the process of being ordered for complete repair of the dish machine.) New Violation. Correct By: 06/04/2024

Raw animal foods (beef, chicken, fish) were observed stored above ready-to-eat foods in the cold drawer and prep unit refrigerators. Raw animal foods must be separated from raw ready-to-eat food such as vegetables during storage. Food items were moved for proper storage at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/21/2024

The chlorine sanitizer concentration for the sanitizing cycle of the dishwasher was 0 parts per million (ppm). Chlorine sanitizing solutions must be between 50-100 ppm. PIC is to properly sanitize equipment in the 3 bay sink until unit is serviced/repaired. New Violation. Correct By: 05/21/2024

Food items in the mini refrigerator were observed held at 47 to 51 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Food items were voluntarily disposed of at the time of inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 05/21/2024

At the time of inspection, the following equipment was observed in need of repair/adjustment... - The dish machine was observed to be leaking sanitizer chemical from the tubing at the top of the dish machine not allowing for a proper mix of sanitizer at the time of inspection. Dish machine is to be serviced for proper sanitizing. - The Mini refrigerator located near the bar area was observed to be maintaining an ambient temperature of 47-48 degrees Fahrenheit with food temps observed maintained at 47 to 51 degrees Fahrenheit. Unit is to be adjusted/repaired for proper cold holding temperatures of 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below. New Violation. Correct By: 05/28/2024

The facility is lacking test strips to measure the concentration of both the 3 Bay Sink Quaternary Ammonium sanitizing solution and for the dish machine Chlorine Sanitizer cycle. Test strips are to be obtained for testing of the sanitizers. New Violation. Correct By: 05/28/2024

The ice machine is lacking an air gap. A plumbing system shall be installed to prevent backflow of a liquid or gas contaminant into the water supply by providing an air gap or installing an approved backflow prevention device. New Violation. Correct By: 05/09/2023

Floor tiles near the hand wash sink in the ware-washing area and kitchen area near dish machine are poor repair. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 08/31/2023

The floor and wall junctures under/near the three bay sink do not have coving. The floor and wall junctures must be coved and closed to no larger than 1/32nd of an inch. New Violation. Correct By: 05/16/2023
